$schema_note: PS-0 authoritative transaction-writer registry. Generated by binding hand-authored analysis to scripts/ps0/discover-writers.mjs output; the analytical fields are human curation, the id set is mechanically matched to discovery so tests/program-studio/ps0-writer-registry.test.mjs stays green. Re-run discovery + regenerate when writers change.
audit_context: docs/design/PROGRAM_STUDIO_ARCHITECTURE.md §9 (Unified Checkout Kernel prerequisite) + §17 (PS-0 row). Output is authoritative for the kernel gate and never stops at a predetermined count.

counts.counts_note: v54 adds public.create_expense/9 and public.receive_stock/6 as keyed idempotent value writers (db_function_writers 41->43, value 38->40). db_triggers_total 131->132 counts the new f2_write_operations immutability guard (reuses app.v41_operation_immutable_guard; no value write, so db_triggers_reaching_value is unchanged). The two former browser-direct value writes to expenses/stock_batches are GONE (browser_direct_writes 28->26, browser_direct_value_writes 2->0): the parallel app agent rewired app/index.html to the new keyed RPCs, so discovery now surfaces browser.rpc:create_expense and browser.rpc:receive_stock instead (browser_rpc_call_sites 101->103, browser_rpc_reaching_value 19->21). identities_total holds at 213 (two browser.write ids removed, two db.fn writers added) and identities_value_impact at 102.

hard_findings: sell_package/3 (non-idempotent, no advisory lock) was execute-granted to authenticated AND called directly by the standalone Packages UI (app/index.html:6539); double-submit duplicated a package sale + client_packages + loyalty earn. [CLOSED by v54: EXECUTE on sell_package(uuid,uuid,uuid) revoked from public/anon/authenticated; only the definer-owned /4 wrapper legitimately calls /3. The standalone UI call has been migrated to the 4-arg overload (now app/index.html:6581, passing a stable idempotency key).]

enroll_membership_v41/3 (non-idempotent passthrough to enroll_membership/3) was granted AND called directly by the standalone Memberships UI (app/index.html:5816); double-submit duplicated a membership + sale. [CLOSED by v54: EXECUTE on enroll_membership_v41(uuid,uuid,uuid) revoked from public/anon/authenticated; only the definer-owned /4 wrapper legitimately calls /3. The standalone UI call has been migrated to the 4-arg overload (now app/index.html:5841, passing a stable idempotency key).]

Browser-direct value writes: app/index.html inserted expenses (P&L) at :7003 and stock_batches (inventory) at :6500 with no RPC and no idempotency (RLS-gated, owner-side). [CLOSED by v54: INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE grants + write-permitting FOR ALL policies revoked/replaced with SELECT-only on both tables; keyed idempotent replacements public.create_expense/9 and public.receive_stock/6 added; the app now calls those RPCs (app/index.html:7054 create_expense, :6533 receive_stock) and no direct expenses/stock_batches write remains.]
medium_findings: redeem_gift_card/4 (and its v41 wrapper called at :5892) has no idempotency key; relies on FOR UPDATE + balance decrement — a retry within remaining balance re-issues credit.
adjust_points/4 (browser :3519) has no idempotency key; a double-submit doubles the manual points adjustment.
record_drawer_movement/6, open_drawer/3, close_drawer/4 have no idempotency (owner-side cash log, not customer value).
kernel_gap_summary: The kernel candidate record_quick_sale already centralises the sale-spine invariants via the sales-table trigger chain (policy snapshot, config version, commission snapshot, FEFO, loyalty earn, reversal guard). ANY unified writer MUST keep inserting sales rows so on_sale_recorded fires exactly once (single earn/qualify event).

record_cart_sale/7 is the no-token line-item writer (server-priced, no discount). PS-1C landed the signed-discount-line + evaluation-token extension as the NEW arity-9 overload record_cart_sale/9 (the kernel finaliser); evaluate_checkout/5 is the evaluation half.

Tender is a separate surface from the accrual spine: record_credit_tender (credit) and record_payment (cash/card/paynow/deposit/no_show_fee) exist but are UI-unwired (finance pilot-disabled). The kernel tender step calls these.

Duplicate/overloaded write paths must be collapsed: sell_package/3 vs /4, enroll_membership_v41/3 vs /4, redeem_reward/4 vs /5, refund_sale vs reverse_sale, and the 3-layer reverse_sale wrapper stack (v40→v34→v20).

Non-sale value writers the kernel must reconcile but not necessarily route through checkout: gift-card issue/redeem, points redeem/reward/expiry, birthday entitlements, campaign grants, package session consumption, credit tender.
Stored value does NOT exist yet (confirmed) — PS-2 introduces it; gift_cards today is single-balance credit, not lot-based.

none — idem key passed at 4088 and 5879

none — v54 CLOSED the former browser-direct expenses INSERT. The write-permitting grant + FOR ALL policy on public.expenses are revoked/replaced with SELECT-only; the parallel app agent rewired app/index.html:7054 to sb.rpc('create_expense', {p_business, p_branch, p_category, p_amount_cents, p_occurred_on, p_supplier, p_description, p_note, p_idempotency_key}) passing a stable idempotency key. This replaces the former browser.write:app/index.html:expenses:INSERT hard finding.

writers.resolves_to: public.receive_stock/6 (idempotent, keyed, audited, cross-tenant-gated via product ownership)
writers.call_site_finding: none — v54 CLOSED the former browser-direct stock_batches INSERT. The write-permitting (default-privilege) grant + FOR ALL policy on public.stock_batches are revoked/replaced with SELECT-only; the parallel app agent rewired app/index.html:6533 to sb.rpc('receive_stock', {p_business, p_product, p_qty, p_expires_on, p_received_on, p_idempotency_key}) passing a stable idempotency key. This replaces the former browser.write:app/index.html:stock_batches:INSERT hard finding.

writers.entry_point: trigger app.on_appointment_completed (AFTER UPDATE on appointments, status→completed)

writers.reversal: the completion sale is reversible via reverse_sale
writers.kernel: NEEDS-WORK — completion is the universal earn event and must route its sale through the kernel; today it inserts sales directly (still gets the trigger chain, but bypasses record_quick_sale idempotency/tender path)

writers.idempotency: NONE explicit — expiry is a set-based sweep of already-expired batches (naturally idempotent: an expired batch is drained once, guarded by remaining>0)

writers.reversal: reversed by an opposite manual adjustment (no dedicated reversal)

writers.rows_written: benefit_fulfilments:INSERT (compensating negative discount fulfilment), budget_commitment_releases:INSERT, budget_periods:UPDATE (committed_cents −= amount); delegates the money/package/sale reversal to public.reverse_sale_v40_base
writers.entry_point: reverse_sale(p_business,p_sale,p_reason,p_idempotency_key,p_reference,p_restock_policy)  [v58 public boundary; extends the v40 boundary with PS-1C discount compensation]

writers.reads: checkout_discount_lines (of the original sale), budget_reservations, budget_periods (FOR UPDATE)
writers.idempotency: the base chain is idem-keyed (financial_operations + package reversal); the compensation is idempotent by the reversal fulfilment canonical key + budget_commitment_releases UNIQUE(reservation_id), so a replayed reversal is a strict no-op
writers.locking: for-update (base sale/staff locks) + budget_periods FOR UPDATE for the release
writers.payment: delegates to reverse_sale_v40_base → v34 → v20 money core: append-only reversing sales row + reversing payments + sale_reversal_audits + sale_reversal_payment_links; credit_ledger compensation
writers.loyalty: compensates earned points/credit via the reversing sale (single-count preserved)
writers.package_membership: restores package sessions (client_packages UPDATE + package_session_reversals) for $0 package sales
writers.reversal: THE reversal engine; append-only compensation, never UPDATE/DELETE of the original; PS-1C adds exact budget release + a signed compensating discount fulfilment (committed = Σreservations − Σreleases >= 0)
writers.kernel: NEEDS-WORK — the reversal path is now a 4-layer wrapper stack (v58 discount compensation → v40 auth → v34 package/provenance → v20 money). The kernel/refund model (REFUND_ARCHITECTURE) must own a single reversal path; keep append-only semantics.
SAFE — manual trigger of the sweep.

writers.kernel: SAFE — status-only, no value movement; stays a lifecycle mutation outside the money kernel.

writers.idempotency: NONE (legacy)
writers.kernel: SAFE (dead path) — execute revoked from authenticated in v34; superseded by /3. Retire in the kernel migration.

writers.idempotency: advisory lock + sale_intent/consumption idempotency; records package_session_consumptions
writers.payment: inserts a $0 sales row (kind=service) so the session counts as a retention visit per policy
writers.loyalty: INDIRECT — the $0 sale fires the earn trigger (visit credit per policy)
writers.reversal: reversible; reverse_sale_v34_base writes package_session_reversals and restores the session
writers.kernel: SAFE — idempotent; grant on the 2-arg legacy overload was revoked (v34). Session consumption is a kernel-adjacent value event to route through the kernel envelope.

writers.entry_point: trigger trg_payment_drawer (AFTER INSERT on payments) → app.on_payment_drawer
writers.payment: mirrors a cash payment into cash_drawer_movements when a drawer session is open
writers.idempotency: one movement per payment row
writers.kernel: SAFE — payments subscriber; keep as a tender side-effect.

writers.entry_point: trigger trg_sale_recorded (AFTER INSERT on sales) → app.on_sale_recorded
writers.reads: sales, sale_policies (v10 resolution), loyalty config snapshot, points_batches, retention taxonomy
writers.sale_policy: YES — resolves counts_as_revenue/visit/earns_points via app.sale_policy_set (v10); re-judges the retention visit window by each historical kind's current policy
writers.config_version: YES — stamps loyalty config version on earn
writers.idempotency: fires once per sales insert; the sales insert itself is the idempotency boundary
writers.loyalty: PRIMARY earn engine — points_ledger + points_batches (or stamps), retention reward_grants, referral qualification (bound to counts_as_visit); credit_ledger for retention-as-credit grants
writers.kernel: SAFE — this is the single earn/qualify subscriber the kernel keeps; do NOT duplicate earning into the kernel (would double-count). Kernel must keep inserting sales so this fires exactly once.

writers.entry_point: trigger trg_sale_stock_deduct (AFTER INSERT on sales) → app.on_sale_stock_deduct
writers.inventory: FEFO auto-deduct from stock_batches when the sale carries product_id/qty
writers.idempotency: inherits the sales-insert idempotency boundary
writers.kernel: SAFE — inventory movement stays a sale subscriber.

writers.entry_point: AFTER INSERT on credit_ledger WHEN entry_type=referral_reward
writers.idempotency: domain_events producer-identity unique + benefit_fulfilments UNIQUE(business,canonical_key), both ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
writers.value: PROMISE ONLY — writes the referral adoption fulfilment (legacy authority holder in shadow, B5); moves no NEW value (the reward credit is the legacy engine's own write)
writers.kernel: N/A — PS-1B fulfilment registry write, not a sale/tender path; the kernel reconciles it as the referral engine's single-count record

writers.rows_written: program_entitlements:INSERT, benefit_fulfilments:INSERT, rule_effect_log:INSERT, benefit_shadow_evaluations:INSERT, event_outbox:INSERT, budget_periods:INSERT/UPDATE, budget_reservations:INSERT, domain_event_execution:INSERT
writers.idempotency: rule_effect_log UNIQUE(event_id,rule_id,effect_index), program_entitlements lazy UNIQUE(business,client,rule,period_key), domain_event_execution marker — all ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
writers.fail_closed: v57 (PS-1B.1): the grant_free_item path consults the TYPED app.ps1b_catalog_price {status,price_cents,reason}; an invalid_kind/not_found/error price (or not_applicable with no amount_cents) logs rule_effect_log outcome 'failed' (failure_reason set) with NO entitlement/fulfilment/reservation — never a silent-zero promise. Each effect runs in its own subtransaction so one poisoned rule fails CLOSED without wedging siblings or the domain_event_execution marker.
writers.value: PROMISE ONLY — materialises claimable entitlements + their fulfilment rows at the TRUE catalog price; consults benefit_registry authority and REFUSES (shadow-logs) unless studio-active; NO credit_ledger/points_ledger/discount/tender write
writers.kernel: N/A in PS-1B (no money moves). PS-1C will route any value-moving effect (grant_credit/discount) through the checkout kernel; here they are shadow-logged only

writers.idempotency: lazy UNIQUE(business,client,rule,period_key) + budget_reservations UNIQUE(period,entitlement); budget row FOR UPDATE serialises the cap check
writers.value: PROMISE ONLY — an issued entitlement keeps its reservation permanently; exhaustion refuses only NEW grants (budget_exhausted)
writers.kernel: N/A — entitlement promise, not a tender; reconciled via benefit_fulfilments

writers.value: PROMISE ONLY — bounded sweep of unexecuted sale.completed/birthday.activated/membership.renewed events
writers.kernel: N/A in PS-1B

writers.entry_point: staff RPC redeem_program_entitlement(uuid,uuid) — member + loyalty module
writers.idempotency: program_entitlement_operations UNIQUE(business,operation_type,idempotency_key); row FOR UPDATE
writers.value: PROMISE ONLY — flips a claimable entitlement to redeemed; NO ledger/tender/discount write (staff-driven claim, no checkout integration)
writers.kernel: N/A — entitlement claim is not a sale; the redeemed item, if it becomes a $0 line, would route through the kernel in a later phase

writers.idempotency: program_entitlement_operations UNIQUE(business,operation_type,idempotency_key); mandatory reason; row FOR UPDATE
writers.value: PROMISE ONLY — reverses an entitlement promise; NO value movement
writers.kernel: N/A

writers.callers: browser till checkout (server-priced quote; the client holds only the opaque token id)
writers.reads: services/products (server prices via app.ps1b_catalog_price), program_rules_compiled (sale.completed discount rules), budget_periods (PROJECTED cap), businesses (active_config_version_id + GST)
writers.idempotency: checkout_evaluation_operations UNIQUE(business_id, idempotency_key) + request_hash; advisory xact lock; replay returns the same token while unexpired+unconsumed, else 22023 stale
writers.value: NON-VALUE — mints a priced quote only; no money, ledger, tender or discount is applied here
writers.kernel_migratability: KERNEL — the server-owned evaluation-token authority; nothing is client-priceable (a price/amount key on any line is a hard error)
writers.kernel: KERNEL — PS-1C evaluation half of the unified checkout kernel.

writers.rows_written: sale_items:INSERT (server lines + signed studio_discount lines), checkout_discount_lines:INSERT, benefit_fulfilments:INSERT, budget_reservations:INSERT, budget_periods:INSERT/UPDATE, checkout_evaluations:UPDATE (consume)
writers.reads: checkout_evaluations (FOR UPDATE), services/products (price re-resolution → cart_hash), budget_periods (FOR UPDATE re-check), financial_operations (replay proof), program_rules (discount label)
writers.sale_policy: INDIRECT — delegates the money write to record_quick_sale; the parent sale carries the discounted total so points/retention/referral earn on it
writers.config_version: atomic revalidation asserts businesses.active_config_version_id is UNCHANGED since evaluation
writers.idempotency: reuses record_quick_sale's financial_operations op-ledger for the parent sale; the token's single-use (consumed_at write-once) governs the discount/provenance/budget writes; a same-token replay returns duplicate_ignored
writers.locking: checkout_evaluations FOR UPDATE (single-use) + budget_periods FOR UPDATE in (rule_id, period_start) order (deadlock-free); any drift → stale_evaluation (P0001)
writers.payment: delegates to record_quick_sale (accrual, p_paid); tender is a separate later surface
writers.loyalty: INDIRECT via the parent sale trigger on the DISCOUNTED amount_cents (correct)
writers.reporting: sales.amount_cents = subtotal − Σ discounts; sale_items reconcile byte-for-byte; get_checkout_discount_report + benefit_fulfilments reconcile
writers.reversal: reverse_sale releases the budget commitment and writes a compensating (negative) fulfilment per discount
writers.value: VALUE — the ONLY path that applies a Studio discount to money; writes the signed discount line + provenance
writers.kernel_migratability: KERNEL — the finalisation half of the unified checkout kernel (v51 record_cart_sale extended with the signed discount line + evaluation token, per architecture §9)
writers.kernel: KERNEL — PS-1C finalisation; the single authoritative discount-applying write path.

writers.entry_point: reverse_sale_v40_base (the v40 auth/package boundary; renamed from reverse_sale in v58 so the v58 outer reverse_sale can compensate checkout discounts)
writers.idempotency: delegates to the v34/v20 base chain (financial_operations + package reversal idem)
writers.value: the retained v40 auth/package-reversal boundary; the compensating checkout-discount + budget-release logic lives in the NEW public.reverse_sale wrapper above it
writers.kernel_migratability: NEEDS-WORK — one internal layer of the 4-deep reversal stack (v58 wrapper → v40 base → v34 base → v20 money core); the refund model must eventually collapse these into one kernel reversal path
writers.kernel: NEEDS-WORK — internal reversal layer; collapse into one kernel reversal path.

PS-1B event-envelope producer: writes ONLY domain_events (append-only immutable log; non-value), deduped by producer identity UNIQUE(business,event_type,source_operation_id,schema_version). Not a customer-value writer.

PS-1B delivery sweep: writes ONLY event_outbox (delivery state) + captured_messages (synthetic-recipient capture/test provider, hard CHECK forbids real recipients). No real message delivery; non-value.
PS-1B referral SHADOW evaluator: writes ONLY benefit_shadow_evaluations (would-be effects). Never benefit_fulfilments, never a value table. Comparator diffs it vs the live referral fulfilment rows.
